_note: Status 'mapped' means the component has been mapped to this criterion against internal platform standards only. No WCAG or external compliance claim is made. Run audit-rules.json checks and update validation_status before changing overall_status. See analysis.md for full risk and token finding details.

standards.name: Platform Design System — Slider Component Standards
standards._note: Internal platform rules for the Slider component. IDs are stable. See standards/platform-code.standards.json for full definitions.

criteria.title: Component must use design tokens for all visual values
criteria.evidence: slider.css references var(--slider-*) component-scoped tokens exclusively. However, no slider-scoped tokens exist in token.css at this time. All token references use Figma manifest values as fallbacks. The CSS architecture is correct; the token definitions are missing. See analysis.md §Token Findings.

criteria.title: RTL layout support is mandatory
criteria.evidence: slider.css implements [dir='rtl'] selectors that reverse fill direction, value position, label alignment, and helper text alignment. RTL was confirmed as a meaningful implementation axis from the manifest (produces real CSS differences). Native input[type='range'] direction is set via CSS direction property in RTL context.

criteria.title: Semantic HTML element required
criteria.evidence: input[type='range'] is the required element for the slider control. It provides keyboard navigation (arrow keys), screen reader role 'slider', and aria-valuemin/max/now automatically. slider.contract.md §HTML Contract documents the anatomy. SLIDER-STRUCT-001 enforces this at the DOM level.

criteria.title: Arabic language font support required
criteria.evidence: slider.css applies font-family via var(--slider-label-font-family) which falls back to 'IBM Plex Sans Arabic' as specified in the Figma manifest. All text elements (label, value, helper) inherit this font.

criteria.title: Minimum touch target 24×24px for interactive elements
criteria.evidence: Small thumb is 12px, Medium thumb is 16px. Both are below 24px visual size. However, input[type='range'] touch target is expanded via padding and the input element itself spans the full track width, providing an adequate click/touch area. The visual thumb size differs from the interactive area. Flagged for design review.

standards.name: Platform Accessibility Internal Standards
standards._note: Accessibility-specific internal standards. See standards/accessibility.standards.json for full definitions.

criteria.title: Keyboard interaction must be fully supported
criteria.evidence: input[type='range'] provides native keyboard interaction: Arrow Left/Right/Up/Down adjust value, Home/End jump to min/max. No custom keyboard handling required. Documented in slider.contract.md §ARIA Contract.

criteria.title: Range slider must provide distinct labels for each thumb
criteria.evidence: Range mode requires aria-label='Minimum value' and aria-label='Maximum value' (or Arabic equivalents) on each input. Documented in slider.contract.md §ARIA Contract and §HTML Contract. SLIDER-A11Y-002 enforces at audit time.

criteria.title: Focus ring must not be suppressed
criteria.evidence: slider.css defines a focus ring via input:focus-visible outline. Native browser outline is overridden and replaced with a custom platform-branded focus indicator using --slider-focus-ring-color. :focus-visible is used (keyboard-only) per platform convention. No Focus Ring layer exists in the Figma manifest — this is an implementation addition from platform accessibility standards.

criteria.title: No hardcoded color, spacing, or radius values in component CSS
criteria.evidence: slider.css contains no hardcoded hex, rgb(), or hsl() values in production selectors. All visual properties reference var(--slider-*) semantic tokens. However, those tokens are not yet defined in token.css — fallback values in the var() calls reference Figma manifest values. The CSS structure is compliant; the token definitions are the blocking gap. SLIDER-TOKEN-001 will pass once token.css is updated.

criteria.title: Disabled state must be visually distinct and non-interactive
criteria.evidence: slider.css applies opacity var(--slider-disabled-opacity, 0.4) and pointer-events: none to .slider[aria-disabled='true']. The input[type='range'] also receives the native disabled attribute. SLIDER-A11Y-005 enforces both conditions at audit.

variant_compliance_notes.slider--sm (small): Thumb visual diameter is 12px, below 24px. Native input click/drag area spans full track width. Design team must confirm whether 12px thumb is acceptable or should be raised to minimum 24px.
variant_compliance_notes.slider--md (medium): Thumb visual diameter is 16px, below 24px. Same caveat as small. Click/drag area spans full track width.
variant_compliance_notes.slider--range: Range mode requires JavaScript for fill positioning and z-index management. Static showcase uses inline style placeholders. JavaScript contract documented in slider.contract.md §JavaScript Contract.
variant_compliance_notes.rtl: RTL fill direction requires JavaScript calculation adjustment (inverted percentage). Documented in slider.contract.md §RTL Behavior.
variant_compliance_notes.disabled (all variants): Low contrast of the disabled state is intentional. Inactive UI components are exempt from active-state contrast requirements per internal accessibility standards.
